rows.name: Veto Rule
target_situations: a required approval or non-negotiable requirement blocks launch
an apparently strong option misses a mandatory gate
rows.action_cue: Name the requirement, owner, evidence, and clearance or waiver condition.
rows.evidence_boundary: Name the requirement, accountable owner, evidence, and clearance or waiver condition.
rows.misuse_warning: Can turn preferences into false hard stops when authority and evidence are missing.
rows.decision_reason: Useful for true required gates, but unsafe for default search until caveat display is guaranteed.
rows.plain_language_fallback: The launch is blocked until the required approval is cleared or waived.
rows.revisit_trigger: Caveat display and lifecycle filters are implemented for docs-catalog preview.

rows.name: Team as roadblock
target_situations: a team is described as blocking another team
dependency language turns people into obstacles
rows.evidence_boundary: Name the blocked dependency, owner, and decision path.
rows.misuse_warning: Turns people into obstacles and hides ownership or system design.
rows.decision_reason: People-as-obstacles framing dehumanizes teams and launders blame.
rows.plain_language_fallback: The dependency is blocked by unresolved ownership or a decision path.
rows.revisit_trigger: Only reopen if the target is a non-human structural dependency.

rows.name: Bag-of-chips story as excuse
target_situations: an empathy story is used after facts establish harm
a perspective-taking story is used to avoid repair
rows.evidence_boundary: Use uncertainty stories only while intent and facts are unresolved.
rows.misuse_warning: Can erase established harm and delay repair or ownership.
rows.decision_reason: Perspective stories are useful before facts are known; after harm is established they can become empathy erasers.
rows.plain_language_fallback: Facts are now known; name harm, repair, and ownership.
rows.revisit_trigger: Use only when facts and intent are still unresolved.

rows.name: Theme Swimlanes
target_situations: a program leader wants three contribution lanes under one customer promise
teams need to map work into a few themes without losing ownership
rows.action_cue: Name one customer promise, two to four contribution lanes, each lane owner, each measure, and explicit exclusions.
rows.evidence_boundary: Check whether each theme has an owner, measure, and customer promise.
rows.misuse_warning: Can become slogan compression when ownership, measures, or tradeoffs are missing.
rows.decision_reason: Locally useful pattern needs real pilot records and role gates before accepted catalog or default search.
rows.plain_language_fallback: These are slogans until ownership and measures make them decision lanes.
rows.revisit_trigger: A real pilot records changed or explicitly unchanged decisions with role-gate closeout.
